PARTIES TO THE ACTION

Plaintiff:
Amara Andersson
Role: Plaintiff
State of Incorporation: Delaware
Principal Place of Business: New York, NY
Jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, SDNY

Defendant:
Sarah Singh
Role: Defendant
State of Incorporation: California
Principal Place of Business: San Francisco, CA
Jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, SDNY

SUMMARY OF COMPLAINT

Plaintiff AtlasCore Technologies LLC brings this action against Defendant SilverBridge Software Group Inc. for alleged breach of contract, misappropriation of trade secrets under the Defend Trade Secrets Act (18 U.S.C. Sec. 1836), and tortious interference with prospective business relations.

AtlasCore alleges that SilverBridge, following the termination of a three-year SaaS partnership agreement executed on January 15, 2021, wrongfully retained and exploited proprietary API integration specifications belonging to AtlasCore. Specifically, Plaintiff contends that Defendant used those specifications to develop a competing product that launched in the market on June 1, 2024, directly undercutting AtlasCore's core revenue stream.

Plaintiff seeks compensatory damages in excess of $4.5 million, punitive damages, a permanent injunction preventing further use of the misappropriated materials, and attorney's fees and costs.

# Ingrid Ivanov — Release Notes

## Version 4.7.2
Release Date: 2024-05-25

### Overview
This is a patch release addressing three customer-reported bugs introduced in v4.7.0. No new features are included. This release does NOT introduce any breaking changes to the public API; all existing integrations will continue to function without modification.

**Minimum Runtime Requirement:** Node.js >= 18.0.0

### Bug Fixes

- **NDS-74044** — Fixed a race condition in the batch upload queue that caused duplicate record entries when network latency exceeded 3 seconds.
- **NDS-94108** — Resolved an unhandled promise rejection thrown when attempting to sync empty collections, which previously caused the sync daemon to crash silently.
- **NDS-74303** — Corrected an off-by-one error in pagination that caused the final page of results to be skipped during full-database export operations.

### Upgrade Instructions
Update using your package manager:
```
npm install nexus-data-sync@4.7.2
```
No configuration changes are required. The changelog for all prior versions is available at https://docs.nexusync.io/changelog.

*Published by the Nexus Engineering Team.*

bugFixes.summary: Fixed a race condition in the batch upload queue that caused duplicate record entries when network latency exceeded 3 seconds.
Resolved an unhandled promise rejection thrown when attempting to sync empty collections, which previously caused the sync daemon to crash silently.
Corrected an off-by-one error in pagination that caused the final page of results to be skipped during full-database export operations.

RESEARCH GRANT PROPOSAL
=======================
Proposal ID: NSF-2024-BIO-18285
Title: Neuroplasticity Mechanisms Underlying Language Acquisition in Bilingual Adults
Submission Date: 2024-10-31
Funding Agency: National Science Foundation
Grant Program: Cognitive Neuroscience Research Initiative (CNRI)
Requested Amount: $487,500 USD
Project Duration: 36 months

ABSTRACT
This proposal investigates the cortical reorganization mechanisms that enable bilingual adults to acquire a third language with significantly reduced effort compared to monolinguals. Utilizing high-density EEG, fMRI, and behavioral assessments across 180 participants, we aim to map the neural correlates of accelerated language acquisition and identify modifiable cognitive predictors. Findings could transform second-language education policy and therapeutic interventions for aphasia recovery.

RESEARCH TEAM
- Pavel Park | Principal Investigator | Stanford University | msqdb@gmail.com
- Olga Andersson | Co-Investigator | UC Berkeley | dgnkj@mail.com
- Sofia Larsson | Research Coordinator | Stanford University | tekcm@university.edu

RESEARCH OBJECTIVES
Obj-46666: Map cortical connectivity differences between bilinguals and monolinguals during L3 acquisition tasks.
Hypothesis: Bilinguals exhibit greater interhemispheric connectivity in frontal language areas.
Expected outcome: Identification of 3+ distinct connectivity signatures correlated with acquisition speed.

Obj-14288: Identify behavioral and cognitive predictors of accelerated L3 acquisition.
Hypothesis: Working memory capacity and phonological awareness mediate the bilingual advantage.
Expected outcome: Validated prediction model with AUC > 0.80.

BUDGET BREAKDOWN
+--------------------+------------+
| Category           | Amount     |
+--------------------+------------+
| Personnel          | $310,000   |
| Equipment and MRI  | $85,000    |
| Participant stipends| $42,500   |
| Travel and Dissem. | $25,000    |
| Indirect Costs     | $25,000    |
+--------------------+------------+

Ethics Review: Approved by Stanford IRB (Protocol #IRB-2024-7731).
Keywords: neuroplasticity, bilingualism, language acquisition, fMRI, EEG, cognitive neuroscience

CLINICAL TRIAL PROTOCOL
Protocol Version 2.1 — Final

Protocol ID: CT-2025-ONCL-93717
Study Title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase III Trial Evaluating the Efficacy and Safety of Velaritinib in Patients with Advanced Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (NSCLC)

Sponsor Organization: Meridian BioTherapeutics, Inc.
Principal Investigator: Prof. Sandra K. Hollingsworth, MD, PhD
Affiliation: Mount Calloway Cancer Institute, Seattle, WA

STUDY DESIGN:
This is a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study. Eligible patients will be randomized 2:1 (active:placebo) to receive either Velaritinib 200 mg orally once daily or matched placebo for 24 months or until disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, or withdrawal of consent.

INTERVENTION:
Velaritinib (VLT-200), a novel selective EGFR/MET dual inhibitor, administered as 200 mg oral tablet once daily. Comparator: matching placebo tablet.

TARGET ENROLLMENT: 679 patients across 35 investigational sites in 8 countries.

STUDY DURATION: Approximately 36 months (24-month treatment period + 12-month follow-up).

ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA:
Inclusion:
  • Confirmed diagnosis of advanced non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), Stage IIIB/IV
  • Age 30–80 years
  • ECOG Performance Status 0–2
  • Adequate organ function as per laboratory parameters

Exclusion:
  • Prior treatment with any EGFR or MET inhibitor
  • Active CNS metastases requiring immediate treatment
  • Severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh C)

PRIMARY ENDPOINT:
Phase: Efficacy
Metric: Progression-Free Survival (PFS)
Timepoint: 12 months post-randomization

SECONDARY ENDPOINTS include Overall Survival (OS), Objective Response Rate (ORR), Duration of Response (DoR), and Patient-Reported Outcomes (PROs).

All procedures are conducted in accordance with ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice guidelines and the Declaration of Helsinki.

# Wei Thompson — Release Notes

## Version 4.7.2
Release Date: 2023-01-07

### Overview
This is a patch release addressing three customer-reported bugs introduced in v4.7.0. No new features are included. This release does NOT introduce any breaking changes to the public API; all existing integrations will continue to function without modification.

**Minimum Runtime Requirement:** Node.js >= 18.0.0

### Bug Fixes

- **NDS-76287** — Fixed a race condition in the batch upload queue that caused duplicate record entries when network latency exceeded 3 seconds.
- **NDS-69770** — Resolved an unhandled promise rejection thrown when attempting to sync empty collections, which previously caused the sync daemon to crash silently.
- **NDS-58916** — Corrected an off-by-one error in pagination that caused the final page of results to be skipped during full-database export operations.

### Upgrade Instructions
Update using your package manager:
```
npm install nexus-data-sync@4.7.2
```
No configuration changes are required. The changelog for all prior versions is available at https://docs.nexusync.io/changelog.

*Published by the Nexus Engineering Team.*

UNITED STATES PATENT APPLICATION
Application Number: US2025/0184732
Filing Date: 2025-05-17
Status: Pending

---

TITLE OF INVENTION:
Adaptive Quantum-Classical Hybrid Inference Engine for Edge-Deployed Neural Network Acceleration

APPLICANT:
Wei Johansson
4000 Innovation Drive, Boulder, CO 80301

INVENTORS:
1. Oscar Larsson, Japanese national
   Primary architect of the hybrid inference scheduling algorithm.
2. Pavel Patel, United States national
   Lead designer of the quantum error-mitigation subsystem.
3. Amara Martinez, German national
   Responsible for the classical co-processor integration framework.

PRIORITY CLAIM:
This application claims priority to provisional application US2024/0923847 filed November 14, 2024.

INTERNATIONAL PATENT CLASSIFICATION:
IPC: G06N 10/40, G06N 3/063

FIELD OF THE TECHNOLOGY:
This invention relates to hybrid quantum-classical computing architectures, and more particularly to inference engines that dynamically partition neural network workloads between quantum processing units (QPUs) and classical co-processors for low-latency edge deployment.

BACKGROUND:
Edge AI deployment faces a fundamental tension: classical neural network inference at the edge is constrained by power and thermal budgets, while cloud offloading introduces unacceptable latency for real-time applications. Pure quantum approaches remain impractical due to qubit coherence limitations and lack of programmable error correction at scale. Existing hybrid systems apply a fixed partition strategy that cannot adapt to changing workload characteristics or quantum device availability, resulting in suboptimal throughput and energy efficiency.

SUMMARY OF THE INVENTION:
The present invention provides a reconfigurable hybrid inference engine comprising a dynamic workload partitioner, a quantum-classical interface (QCI) bus, and an on-device reinforcement learning scheduler that continuously optimizes the partition boundary based on real-time coherence telemetry and task-level latency requirements. The invention achieves up to 3.8× improvement in inference throughput and 42% reduction in energy per inference compared to classical-only baselines on convolutional and transformer workloads at the edge.

ABSTRACT:
A hybrid quantum-classical inference engine for neural network acceleration at the edge. The engine comprises a dynamic workload partitioner that assigns tensor operations to a quantum processing unit (QPU) or classical co-processor based on real-time coherence telemetry, a quantum-classical interface bus enabling sub-microsecond handoff, and an on-device reinforcement learning scheduler that optimizes partitioning policy. The invention achieves significant improvements in inference throughput and energy efficiency over classical-only and fixed-partition hybrid approaches, enabling deployment on resource-constrained edge devices.

CLAIMS:
Claim 1 [Independent]: A hybrid inference engine comprising: a quantum processing unit (QPU); a classical co-processor; a dynamic workload partitioner configured to assign tensor operations from a neural network inference task to either the QPU or the classical co-processor based on real-time quantum coherence telemetry; and a reinforcement learning scheduler executing on the classical co-processor and configured to update the partitioning policy based on measured latency and energy metrics.

Claim 2 [Dependent]: The hybrid inference engine of Claim 1, wherein the dynamic workload partitioner comprises a coherence telemetry module configured to sample QPU T2 relaxation time at intervals not exceeding 100 microseconds.

Claim 3 [Dependent]: The hybrid inference engine of Claim 1, wherein the quantum-classical interface bus supports bidirectional tensor transfer with end-to-end latency not exceeding 800 nanoseconds.

Claim 4 [Independent]: A method of performing neural network inference using a hybrid quantum-classical system, the method comprising: receiving an inference task comprising a plurality of tensor operations; sampling real-time coherence telemetry from a quantum processing unit; assigning each tensor operation to either the quantum processing unit or a classical co-processor using a learned partitioning policy; executing the assigned operations; and updating the partitioning policy using a reinforcement learning reward signal derived from measured inference latency and energy consumption.

PRIOR ART REFERENCES:
Ref. [ID-811786]: Patent. Relevant as prior art teaching static quantum-classical partitioning for matrix multiplication; does not address dynamic reallocation or RL-based scheduling.
Ref. [ID-170800]: Journal Article. Addresses quantum neural networks on NISQ devices; no edge deployment or classical co-processor integration discussed.
Ref. [ID-391908]: Conference Paper. Presents an edge AI accelerator chip with fixed-function classical inference; no quantum co-processing component.

FIGURES:
Fig. 1: System architecture overview showing QPU, classical co-processor, QCI bus, and RL scheduler interconnects.
Fig. 2: Flowchart of the dynamic workload partitioning algorithm.
Fig. 3: Coherence telemetry sampling pipeline and T2 estimation method.
Fig. 4: Comparative throughput benchmark results across three workload types (CNN, Transformer, GNN).
Fig. 5: Energy-per-inference curves comparing the invention against classical-only and fixed-partition baselines.

IN THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT
FOR THE SOUTHERN DISTRICT OF NEW YORK

CASE FILING HEADER
------------------
Case Number: 1:2024-cv-09832-LTS
Date of Filing: September 9, 2024

PARTIES TO THE ACTION

Plaintiff:
Kenji Okafor
Role: Plaintiff
State of Incorporation: Delaware
Principal Place of Business: New York, NY
Jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, SDNY

Defendant:
Luna Huang
Role: Defendant
State of Incorporation: California
Principal Place of Business: San Francisco, CA
Jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, SDNY

SUMMARY OF COMPLAINT

Plaintiff AtlasCore Technologies LLC brings this action against Defendant SilverBridge Software Group Inc. for alleged breach of contract, misappropriation of trade secrets under the Defend Trade Secrets Act (18 U.S.C. Sec. 1836), and tortious interference with prospective business relations.

AtlasCore alleges that SilverBridge, following the termination of a three-year SaaS partnership agreement executed on January 15, 2021, wrongfully retained and exploited proprietary API integration specifications belonging to AtlasCore. Specifically, Plaintiff contends that Defendant used those specifications to develop a competing product that launched in the market on June 1, 2024, directly undercutting AtlasCore's core revenue stream.

Plaintiff seeks compensatory damages in excess of $4.5 million, punitive damages, a permanent injunction preventing further use of the misappropriated materials, and attorney's fees and costs.

CLINICAL TRIAL PROTOCOL
Protocol Version 2.1 — Final

Protocol ID: CT-2025-ONCL-0847
Study Title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase III Trial Evaluating the Efficacy and Safety of Velaritinib in Patients with Advanced Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (NSCLC)

Sponsor Organization: Meridian BioTherapeutics, Inc.
Principal Investigator: Prof. Sandra K. Hollingsworth, MD, PhD
Affiliation: Mount Calloway Cancer Institute, Seattle, WA

STUDY DESIGN:
This is a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study. Eligible patients will be randomized 2:1 (active:placebo) to receive either Velaritinib 200 mg orally once daily or matched placebo for 24 months or until disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, or withdrawal of consent.

INTERVENTION:
Velaritinib (VLT-200), a novel selective EGFR/MET dual inhibitor, administered as 200 mg oral tablet once daily. Comparator: matching placebo tablet.

TARGET ENROLLMENT: 480 patients across 35 investigational sites in 8 countries.

STUDY DURATION: Approximately 36 months (24-month treatment period + 12-month follow-up).

ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA:
Inclusion:
  • Confirmed diagnosis of advanced non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), Stage IIIB/IV
  • Age 30–80 years
  • ECOG Performance Status 0–2
  • Adequate organ function as per laboratory parameters

Exclusion:
  • Prior treatment with any EGFR or MET inhibitor
  • Active CNS metastases requiring immediate treatment
  • Severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh C)

PRIMARY ENDPOINT:
Phase: Efficacy
Metric: Progression-Free Survival (PFS)
Timepoint: 12 months post-randomization

SECONDARY ENDPOINTS include Overall Survival (OS), Objective Response Rate (ORR), Duration of Response (DoR), and Patient-Reported Outcomes (PROs).

All procedures are conducted in accordance with ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice guidelines and the Declaration of Helsinki.

# Olga Garcia — Release Notes

## Version 4.7.2
Release Date: 2023-06-08

### Overview
This is a patch release addressing three customer-reported bugs introduced in v4.7.0. No new features are included. This release does NOT introduce any breaking changes to the public API; all existing integrations will continue to function without modification.

**Minimum Runtime Requirement:** Node.js >= 18.0.0

### Bug Fixes

- **NDS-59786** — Fixed a race condition in the batch upload queue that caused duplicate record entries when network latency exceeded 3 seconds.
- **NDS-84683** — Resolved an unhandled promise rejection thrown when attempting to sync empty collections, which previously caused the sync daemon to crash silently.
- **NDS-42208** — Corrected an off-by-one error in pagination that caused the final page of results to be skipped during full-database export operations.

### Upgrade Instructions
Update using your package manager:
```
npm install nexus-data-sync@4.7.2
```
No configuration changes are required. The changelog for all prior versions is available at https://docs.nexusync.io/changelog.

*Published by the Nexus Engineering Team.*

bugFixes.summary: Fixed a race condition in the batch upload queue that caused duplicate record entries when network latency exceeded 3 seconds.
Resolved an unhandled promise rejection thrown when attempting to sync empty collections, which previously caused the sync daemon to crash silently.
Corrected an off-by-one error in pagination that caused the final page of results to be skipped during full-database export operations.

UNITED STATES PATENT APPLICATION
Application Number: US2025/0184732
Filing Date: 2025-05-19

TITLE OF INVENTION:
Adaptive Quantum-Classical Hybrid Inference Engine for Edge-Deployed Neural Network Acceleration

APPLICANT:
QuantaLogic Systems Corporation
4000 Innovation Drive, Boulder, CO 80301

INVENTORS:
1. Dr. Yuki Tanaka, Japanese national
   Primary architect of the hybrid inference scheduling algorithm.
2. Dr. Faisal Al-Rashidi, United States national
   Lead designer of the quantum error-mitigation subsystem.
3. Ms. Caroline Voss, German national
   Responsible for the classical co-processor integration framework.

PRIORITY CLAIM:
This application claims priority to provisional application US2024/0923847 filed November 14, 2024.

INTERNATIONAL PATENT CLASSIFICATION:
IPC: G06N 10/40, G06N 3/063

FIELD OF THE TECHNOLOGY:
This invention relates to hybrid quantum-classical computing architectures, and more particularly to inference engines that dynamically partition neural network workloads between quantum processing units (QPUs) and classical co-processors for low-latency edge deployment.

BACKGROUND:
Edge AI deployment faces a fundamental tension: classical neural network inference at the edge is constrained by power and thermal budgets, while cloud offloading introduces unacceptable latency for real-time applications. Pure quantum approaches remain impractical due to qubit coherence limitations and lack of programmable error correction at scale. Existing hybrid systems apply a fixed partition strategy that cannot adapt to changing workload characteristics or quantum device availability, resulting in suboptimal throughput and energy efficiency.

SUMMARY OF THE INVENTION:
The present invention provides a reconfigurable hybrid inference engine comprising a dynamic workload partitioner, a quantum-classical interface (QCI) bus, and an on-device reinforcement learning scheduler that continuously optimizes the partition boundary based on real-time coherence telemetry and task-level latency requirements. The invention achieves up to 3.8× improvement in inference throughput and 42% reduction in energy per inference compared to classical-only baselines on convolutional and transformer workloads at the edge.

ABSTRACT:
A hybrid quantum-classical inference engine for neural network acceleration at the edge. The engine comprises a dynamic workload partitioner that assigns tensor operations to a quantum processing unit (QPU) or classical co-processor based on real-time coherence telemetry, a quantum-classical interface bus enabling sub-microsecond handoff, and an on-device reinforcement learning scheduler that optimizes partitioning policy. The invention achieves significant improvements in inference throughput and energy efficiency over classical-only and fixed-partition hybrid approaches, enabling deployment on resource-constrained edge devices.

CLAIMS:
Claim 1 [Independent]: A hybrid inference engine comprising: a quantum processing unit (QPU); a classical co-processor; a dynamic workload partitioner configured to assign tensor operations from a neural network inference task to either the QPU or the classical co-processor based on real-time quantum coherence telemetry; and a reinforcement learning scheduler executing on the classical co-processor and configured to update the partitioning policy based on measured latency and energy metrics.

Claim 2 [Dependent]: The hybrid inference engine of Claim 1, wherein the dynamic workload partitioner comprises a coherence telemetry module configured to sample QPU T2 relaxation time at intervals not exceeding 100 microseconds.

Claim 3 [Dependent]: The hybrid inference engine of Claim 1, wherein the quantum-classical interface bus supports bidirectional tensor transfer with end-to-end latency not exceeding 800 nanoseconds.

Claim 4 [Independent]: A method of performing neural network inference using a hybrid quantum-classical system, the method comprising: receiving an inference task comprising a plurality of tensor operations; sampling real-time coherence telemetry from a quantum processing unit; assigning each tensor operation to either the quantum processing unit or a classical co-processor using a learned partitioning policy; executing the assigned operations; and updating the partitioning policy using a reinforcement learning reward signal derived from measured inference latency and energy consumption.

PRIOR ART REFERENCES:
Ref. [US10,832,155 B2]: Patent. Relevant as prior art teaching static quantum-classical partitioning for matrix multiplication; does not address dynamic reallocation or RL-based scheduling.
Ref. [arXiv:2312.04819]: Journal Article. Addresses quantum neural networks on NISQ devices; no edge deployment or classical co-processor integration discussed.
Ref. [IEEE ISSCC 2024, Paper 18.3]: Conference Paper. Presents an edge AI accelerator chip with fixed-function classical inference; no quantum co-processing component.

FIGURES:
Fig. 1: System architecture overview showing QPU, classical co-processor, QCI bus, and RL scheduler interconnects.
Fig. 2: Flowchart of the dynamic workload partitioning algorithm.
Fig. 3: Coherence telemetry sampling pipeline and T2 estimation method.
Fig. 4: Comparative throughput benchmark results across three workload types (CNN, Transformer, GNN).
Fig. 5: Energy-per-inference curves comparing the invention against classical-only and fixed-partition baselines.

Service: Diego Andersson
Version: v3.2.1
Base URL: https://api.datastreamhq.io/v3

AUTHENTICATION
--------------
All requests must include an API key in the Authorization header using the format:
  Authorization: ApiKey <your_key_here>
Authentication method: API_KEY. Keys can be generated in the Developer Console.

RATE LIMITS
-----------
Free Tier:    60 requests/minute, 10,000 requests/day
Pro Tier:     300 requests/minute, 100,000 requests/day
Enterprise:   Custom limits -- contact sales@datastreamhq.io

Default rate limit applied to all accounts unless upgraded: 60 requests per minute, 10,000 per day.

ENDPOINTS
---------

1. GET /events
   Retrieve a paginated list of analytics events for the authenticated account.
   Auth required: Yes

2. POST /events
   Submit one or more new events to the ingestion pipeline.
   Payload: JSON array of event objects (see schema below).
   Auth required: Yes

3. GET /reports/{reportId}
   Fetch a pre-generated report by its unique identifier.
   Reports older than 90 days are automatically archived.
   Auth required: Yes

4. DELETE /events/{eventId}
   Remove a specific event from the dataset. This action is irreversible.
   Auth required: Yes

5. GET /health
   Public health check endpoint -- returns system status without authentication.
   Auth required: No

For SDK clients and code samples in Python, JavaScript, and Go, visit:
https://docs.datastreamhq.io/sdks
